scope: DeepSeek-V4 layer-2 S4096 compressed sparse-MLA dKV source-boundary producer experiments on MI350

code_changes.change: Added scalar-contribution, head-loop, and H64 CK contribution branches; fixed MFMA operand validity so sparse row validity zeros A operands only, not shared B operands.
Added query-major selected-tile source-boundary dKV kernel with P/dP tile page, four-head-wave contribution consumer, and shared-memory hash reduction by compressed group.
Added selected_tile_hash_mfma validation so the new external HIP source can run as a full-D source-boundary row-leader dKV producer.
Applied the same MFMA operand-validity fix to the group-panel scorepage contribution path.

key_results.read: MFMA score/prob producer was correct; scalar contribution isolated the bad CK contribution path.
Correct CK H64 contribution tile works once B operands are not zeroed by sparse row validity.
Active-group owner reduces writes but catastrophically replays QK/dO.V for every active-group block.
Selected-tile hash reducer is correct at S512 and benefits from four head waves.
Unsorted K64/T4 has too little duplicate-group locality and the P/dP page path is too expensive.
Sorting improves group locality but not enough; hash/P-dP producer mechanics remain far above target.
Direct global atomics from the same producer show shared hash reduction adds about 14 ms on top of an already slow producer.
No-write mode pins the producer/page/contribution floor at about 47.8 ms.

reject: selected-tile P/dP shared-page plus hash reducer
retain: CK H64 contribution operand fix: sparse validity gates A rows only; B tile loads stay independent
forward-known sorted group sidecar as useful metadata, but not through a materialized P/dP page
decision.next_code_gate: Move the group-aware epilogue into the existing fast query-major scorepage/Triton-style producer without materializing a selected-tile P/dP page: compute score/prob/dP for K16/K64, immediately consume it for dKV, and only keep a tiny per-D local reducer.
